description_long: This object is a classic lever-action shotgun with a long, slender barrel and a wooden full stock. The barrel and receiver are finished in a dark, likely blued metal, while the stock and fore-end are made of polished wood with a rich brown hue. The lever loop is prominent beneath the receiver, indicating the action mechanism, and the trigger guard is rounded and integrated with the lever. The shotgun features a tubular magazine running parallel beneath the barrel, typical of lever-action designs. The overall construction appears robust, with a smooth, glossy finish on both the metal and wood components. The design is utilitarian yet elegant, suitable for both display and functional use in an urban or rural context.
description: A lever-action shotgun with a long blued barrel, wooden full stock, and a prominent lever loop beneath the receiver. The finish is glossy and the design is classic and robust.
description_view_0: The shotgun is viewed directly from the muzzle end, showing the narrow profile of the barrel and the alignment of the stock behind it.
description_view_1: A full side view reveals the long barrel, wooden full stock, lever loop, and tubular magazine beneath the barrel.
description_view_2: Viewed from the butt end, the shotgun appears as a slim vertical line, emphasizing the narrowness of the barrel and stock.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the full length of the barrel, stock, and lever loop, with similar details visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long and round with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: Rounded, medium-sized trigger guard integrated with the lever, smooth with no finger grooves or reinforcement
